priorities: Channeling capital into measurable rainforest restoration and indigenous land protection
Aligning investment returns with philanthropic impact
redLines: Investments in companies that treat nature as a free resource
Any exposure to palm‑oil linked deforestation or shell‑company supply‑chain loopholes
Green‑washed ESG metrics without verified biodiversity or supply‑chain data
preferredSectors: Enterprises with verified, eco‑friendly supply chains
avoidedSectors: Palm‑oil producers and related commodity chains
Industries with high deforestation risk (conventional agriculture, certain mining)
Fossil‑fuel heavy companies lacking clear transition plans
Firms with opaque or unverified ESG reporting

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Long‑term (10+ years) focused on legacy and multi‑generational impact
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Low immediate liquidity needs; prefers to reinvest cash and sweep dividends into foundation accounts

lifeEvents.description: Initial onboarding – wealth defined as an extension of a sustainable‑agriculture exit and philanthropic mission.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Set the overarching sustainability leadership focus for the entire mandate.

lifeEvents.description: Capital call of USD 250,000 for Atlantic Forest land acquisition; trimmed low‑scoring ESG fixed‑income positions.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Increased exposure to high‑impact land‑restoration projects and reinforced positive‑screening discipline.

lifeEvents.description: Field trip to the Amazon; reinforced respect for companies investing in measurable rainforest restoration and indigenous protection.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Strengthened preference for firms with on‑the‑ground conservation activities.

lifeEvents.description: Sub‑account created for the foundation’s youth environmental‑education program.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Added a dedicated vehicle for impact‑focused capital targeting the next generation.

lifeEvents.description: Requested all June dividend payouts be swept to the primary foundation account ahead of a South‑American conservation trip.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Ensured cash flow directly supports ongoing philanthropic projects, reducing idle liquidity.

personalProfile.interests: sustainable agriculture, rainforest restoration, biodiversity conservation, youth environmental education
personalProfile.quirks: insists on real, measurable outcomes; rejects generic ESG products; frequently references Atlantic Forest project
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: mention of Atlantic Forest land acquisition, updates on reforestation in Peru/Brazil, youth education initiatives
personalProfile.avoidTopics: generic investment products, green‑washing narratives, competitor banks

keyQuotes: "I want to actively penalize companies that treat nature as a free resource."
"We expect Sarah to bring the same alignment of values and environmental transparency to our portfolio that Thomas did. No standard, generic investment products."
"If a company takes a brave, absolute stance against palm oil deforestation and actively puts money into reforestation, that is exactly where my money should be."
"Transparency in sourcing makes logical sense to me because companies that don't protect their supply chains from ecological collapse won't survive the next decade anyway."
"We view ourselves as progressive stewards of wealth."
